headline: Sunshine Coast Horse Racing Analysis for Friday, March 6th
analysis:
R1 – Access Insulation Sunshine Coast QTIS 3YO Handicap (1200m)

The leading two runners appear evenly matched in this opening event. 1. Ritualize resumes after a lengthy spell but showed ability in the first two career runs, making this a strong chance fresh. 3. Ave Cantare also returns from a break and recent trial work suggests the runner is ready to perform well. 2. Highgrove Rose comes back to provincial grade and could be competitive with the right run. 4. Unfaithful scored first-up here last time and has proven track form, making it a runner to consider.

Selections: 1. Ritualize (2)  |  2. Ave Cantare (3)  |  3. Highgrove Rose (5)  |  4. Unfaithful (1)

R2 – Kevlacat Power Boats Australia Fillies & Mares Maiden Handicap (1200m)

The race looks likely to come down to the top selections. 7. Icymiss comes from a strong stable and could improve sharply dropping back to this grade. 3. Castlereagh Affair debuts here and from a respected yard should be competitive. 1. Spies On performed well on debut at this track and is capable of further improvement. 4. Cliodhna Maeve has been racing consistently and could surprise with the right run.

Selections: 1. Icymiss (5)  |  2. Castlereagh Affair (4)  |  3. Spies On (7)  |  4. Cliodhna Maeve (8)

R3 – Higgins Coatings Colts, Geldings & Entires Maiden Handicap (1200m)

5. The Aviator showed promise when resuming at Doomben and dropping back to provincial level should make this more manageable. 1. Awarded Difference debuts here and the Matthew Hoysted runner could show ability first-up. 4. Sky Dweller also makes a race debut and comes from a stable capable of producing early winners. 8. Under Arrest returns after a lengthy break and recent trial work suggests readiness.

Selections: 1. The Aviator (4)  |  2. Awarded Difference (10)  |  3. Sky Dweller (12)  |  4. Under Arrest (7)

R4 – Barrier Reef Pools Benchmark 62 Handicap (1800m)

7. Possibilities has been racing consistently with a win and several placings this preparation and looks hard to beat again. 4. Bold Blaze arrives in winning form after consecutive victories and cannot be ignored. 8. Kurithea has been competitive in metro company this campaign and should measure up here. 5. Sacred Bull drops back to a suitable distance and could be in the finish.

Selections: 1. Possibilities (10)  |  2. Bold Blaze (3)  |  3. Kurithea (7)  |  4. Sacred Bull (8)

R5 – Martin Collins Australia Colts, Geldings & Entires Benchmark 60 Handicap (1400m)

The race appears to centre around the top two selections. 12. Chevallum impressed when winning last start and has begun the career strongly. 15. Yes Donald Yes also comes off a maiden victory and should continue to improve with racing. 10. He'sgotthesay resumes from a break but trial form suggests readiness. 17. The Last Saga scored last start and must be respected again.

Selections: 1. Chevallum (13)  |  2. Yes Donald Yes (8)  |  3. He'sgotthesay (1)  |  4. The Last Saga (10)

R6 – TAB Fillies & Mares Benchmark 60 Handicap (1400m)

4. Smudged has been consistent throughout the career and ran well first-up here when narrowly beaten as favourite. 10. Hey Chardonnay also produced a solid run last start at this track and should be competitive again. 1. Retainer arrives off a win and represents a stable capable of producing repeat performances. 5. Decorative has placed fresh before and could fill a minor position.

Selections: 1. Smudged (5)  |  2. Hey Chardonnay (2)  |  3. Retainer (11)  |  4. Decorative (6)

headline: Kensington NSW Race Day Tips — 2026-03-11
analysis:
At Stony Creek under soft conditions in the Melina Bath MP Maiden Plate over 2100m, SNOBBERY is well positioned to build on its recent placing at the same venue. Having shown improvement on a soft track, this entry is primed for a strong performance. MAYEKI, narrowly missing out at long odds at Yarra Valley, has already placed this preparation at Wangaratta and is a contender with a touch of luck. GOLD FRANC, seventh last start at Pakenham, comes from a reputable stable and should be considered for broader betting options. NOBLE FALCON, returning after a nine-week break, could spring a surprise following a recent trial.

In the Meeniyan Hotel After Party Maiden Plate over 1600m, KADDARI commands attention following a placing as the favorite at Werribee. With three placings in seven starts this preparation, the horse is from a respected stable. MR MARGOLIN made an impressive start with a third-place finish at Pakenham and presents a strong case backed by stable form. Making its debut, GUTHRIE comes from a stable with a solid reputation, making it a horse to watch. VELVET CITY, two lengths behind the winner at Pakenham, is a potential threat under the guidance of trainer Nathan McPherson.

The Meeniyan IGA Maiden Plate, spanning 1100m, highlights a competitive field. ZOUSOUTH, showing the speed to overcome a wide barrier, is the top pick with its training strength behind it. UNA GENTLE, with three placings from seven starts this preparation and trained by Shane Nichols and Hayden Black, remains a strong candidate for a spot in the top. LE NOTRE returns from a lengthy spell, with recent trials suggesting a potential surprise performance. HE'S FIERCE, a debutante from Michael Huglin’s stable, poses a significant threat as the real danger.

In the Sen Dr Michael Bourke OAM Sprint over 1100m, JUICE BOX is poised for success after a dominant lead-and-win performance at Sale on a heavy track. With three wins in this campaign, JUICE BOX is well-placed. TRAK CHILLER is in commendable form with consecutive wins at Stony Creek, making it a formidable opponent if in contention. ICHIBERU, with two placings from five starts, holds promise for an each-way bet. AUSSIE ADAPTOR, fresh off breaking its maiden at the same track, offers solid each-way value.

The Mary Aldred MP 0-56 Handicap over 1000m showcases PHANTOM PRINCE, which claimed victory at long odds on a soft track at Stony Creek in its first start back, bearing solid backing from its camp. STEWART is in robust form with two wins this campaign, including a narrow second last time out, suggesting it should not be underestimated. IRRESISTIBLE SIR, racing on a quick turnaround, has shown consistency with three placings in six attempts, maintaining a strong chance. DOMINANT MISS resumes after a spell, bolstered by a successful trial that indicates potential.

In the Tac Rob Meyer 0-56 Handicap over 2100m, GINGER SWEET is favored following a placing at Pakenham on a soft track, with three placings from seven this preparation. REMIGIUS, narrowly missing last time and maintaining a solid record as a favored contender, is also a top choice. BAFFLECK, having placed once this prep, promises to be competitive, while REAL INTRIGUE, returning to non-metro class following a fifth-place finish, remains in contention.

During the Ladbrokes Stony Creek Cup at 2100m, CHASING APHRODITE stands out as a formidable second-up runner, with a victory in one of its seven prior attempts. Returning to non-metro class enhances its prospects as a genuine contender. STATION ONE has demonstrated robust form with three victories from six starts this campaign, bolstered by a recent trial win. REGAL LION, taking a step down from metro level, has secured two wins this campaign, keeping it in the mix. CABLE DANCER, also stepping down, boasts strong camp support that could lead to an upset.

In the Danny O’Brien MP 0-56 Handicap over 1600m, HEY BELLA emerges as a key contender following a third-place finish at Yarra Valley and favorable positioning. AVONVIEW, with two placings from eight starts, continues to show potential, having finished in midfield last time. MARIOTA, following a recent trial, brings with it a strong stable reputation, suggesting credible each-way claims. BEARBRASSBLACKFISH, despite a five-length deficit last start at Moe, under Greg Eurell's watchful eye, remains a viable option.
